platforms.name: Google Vertex AI
details: **Lyria 3 Audio Generation (Public Preview) — March 25, 2026**: Two new AI audio/music generation models added to Vertex AI:
`lyria-3-pro-preview` — generates up to 184 seconds of audio
`lyria-3-clip-preview` — generates up to 30 seconds of audio

findings.title: Vertex AI Lyria 3 Audio Generation Models
findings.description: Google added two new generative AI model endpoints (`lyria-3-pro-preview`, `lyria-3-clip-preview`) to Vertex AI in public preview. These expand the platform's generative surface area beyond text and image into audio/music generation. If enterprise agents or workflows can invoke these models, AISPM should discover and inventory Lyria 3 deployments and track data flows through audio generation endpoints

platforms.name: Azure AI Foundry (now "Microsoft Foundry")
details: **New "Explore more Azure AI" section** added to landing page with cross-references to **"What are Foundry Tools?"** and **"Microsoft Agent Framework"** — indicating new services/concepts in the Foundry ecosystem. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-studio/

**Foundry IQ (preview)** — new knowledge-base-connected agent feature announced in March 2026 what's new. Connects knowledge bases to Foundry Agent Service. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/whats-new-foundry
**Fireworks models (preview)** — new third-party model provider integration on Microsoft Foundry. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/whats-new-foundry
**Hosted agent lifecycle management** — new articles for deploying and managing hosted agents in Foundry Agent Service. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/whats-new-foundry
**Claude Code for Microsoft Foundry** — new integration enabling Claude Code configuration with Microsoft Foundry. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/whats-new-foundry

**Microsoft Agent Framework** — comprehensive agent orchestration framework with support for multiple providers (Foundry, Azure OpenAI, OpenAI, Anthropic, Amazon Bedrock, GitHub Copilot, Ollama) and A2A protocol. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/agent-framework/agents/

platforms.name: Power Apps
details: **New "Vibe" AI copilot experience (preview)** — AI-native platform that builds complete solutions (data models, business logic, UI) from natural language. Available at vibe.powerapps.com. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-apps/vibe/overview

**New "Create a code app"** capability — code-first app creation using React/Vue with Power Platform deployment via `pac code push`. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-apps/developer/code-apps/overview
**New version 26033** rolling out (Make.PowerApps.com), first available Mar 25, 2026. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-platform/released-versions/powerapps

platforms.name: Power Pages
details: **New "Enable agent in your site"** capability — embed AI agents directly into Power Pages sites with site-context awareness and web role-based visibility control. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-pages/getting-started/enable-agent
**New "Force Bing webmaster to index your site"** option added under AI and Copilot section. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-pages/

platforms.name: Windsurf
details: **SKILL.md file support** (v1.9577.24, Mar 9) — custom skill definitions from `.windsurf/skills/` directory with supporting files. Source: https://docs.windsurf.com/windsurf/cascade/skills
**Enterprise MDM-managed system-level skill definitions** — organizations can deploy managed skill sets via MDM policies. Source: https://docs.windsurf.com/windsurf/cascade/skills
**Quota billing system** (v1.9577.43, Mar 19) — new daily/weekly usage metrics within the IDE. Source: https://windsurf.com/changelog

platforms.name: Dia Browser
details: **Bug Bounty Program** launched via HackerOne for responsible vulnerability disclosure. Source: https://diabrowser.com/security
**Expanded infrastructure security details** — role-based production access restrictions, encryption in transit and at rest, regular data collection audits. Source: https://diabrowser.com/security

findings.title: Microsoft Foundry IQ (Preview)
findings.description: New knowledge-base-connected agent feature in Foundry Agent Service. Foundry IQ enables agents to query connected knowledge bases, introducing new entity types: IQ-enabled agents, knowledge base connections, and IQ query sessions. AISPM must discover and inventory Foundry IQ agents and their knowledge base bindings to map data access patterns and ensure sensitive knowledge bases are governed

findings.title: Microsoft Agent Framework
findings.description: New comprehensive agent orchestration framework with support for 11+ inference providers (Foundry, Azure OpenAI, OpenAI, Anthropic, Amazon Bedrock, GitHub Copilot, Ollama) and A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol for cross-agent communication. Introduces new entity types: framework-managed agents, multi-provider agent configurations, A2A proxy agents, and hosted agent deployments. AISPM must discover agents built with this framework across all supported providers and map their tool bindings, MCP connections, and inter-agent communication patterns

findings.title: Power Apps "Vibe" AI Copilot Experience
findings.description: New AI-native app creation experience in Power Apps (preview). Users describe business problems in natural language, and Vibe generates complete solutions including data models, business logic, and UI code. Creates new entity types (Vibe-generated apps, AI-created data models and code components) that need discovery and inventory tracking. AISPM must map Vibe apps as a distinct resource type — they may contain AI-generated code with different security characteristics than manually built apps

findings.title: Power Pages Agent Enablement
findings.description: New capability to embed AI agents within Power Pages sites. Agents automatically gain site-context awareness and can generate responses based on visitor web roles. Introduces agent entities deployed on public-facing web pages with role-based access control. AISPM must discover these agents and AIDR must monitor their data flows given the external exposure surface — agents responding to unauthenticated or low-privilege visitors pose data leakage risk

findings.title: Microsoft Foundry Hosted Agents
findings.description: New managed deployment model for AI agents in Foundry Agent Service. Agents can be deployed as hosted services with lifecycle management (create, deploy, scale, stop). Introduces new infrastructure entities: hosted agent instances, deployment configurations, and service endpoints. AISPM must discover hosted agents and map their model bindings, tool access, and network exposure

findings.title: Fireworks Models on Microsoft Foundry (Preview)
findings.description: New third-party model provider (Fireworks AI) integrated into Microsoft Foundry. Expands the model catalog with Fireworks models. AISPM model inventory must be updated to discover and track deployments using Fireworks models for compliance and governance

findings.title: Foundry Tools (Rebranded Azure AI Services)
findings.description: "Foundry Tools" is the new name for Azure AI Services within the Microsoft Foundry platform. Includes Azure OpenAI, Content Safety, Speech, Vision, Document Intelligence, Language, and Translation services. While the underlying services exist, the rebranding under Foundry means new API endpoints, SDK paths, and resource identifiers that AISPM inventory mappings must be updated to track

findings.title: Power Apps Code Apps
findings.description: New code-first app creation type in Power Apps. Developers build with React/Vue locally and deploy to Power Platform via `pac code push`. These apps have different security characteristics than canvas/model-driven apps — developer-authored code runs within Power Platform's runtime. AISPM inventory should track this new app type distinctly

findings.title: Windsurf SKILL.md and Enterprise MDM Skills
findings.description: Windsurf added SKILL.md file support for custom skill definitions (`.windsurf/skills/`) and MDM-managed system-level skill definitions for Enterprise deployments. Skills can bundle supporting files and define multi-step workflows that Cascade AI executes. Enterprise MDM integration enables organization-wide skill policies deployed to all user machines — endpoint monitoring should track skill definitions and their tool/MCP interactions

findings.title: Claude Code Integration with Microsoft Foundry
findings.description: New documentation for configuring Claude Code as an AI development tool connected to Microsoft Foundry. This creates a new attack surface where Claude Code agents can interact with Foundry resources, models, and deployed agents. AIRT should test for prompt injection and privilege escalation in Claude Code sessions connected to Foundry

platforms.name: Amazon Bedrock
details: **New open-weight models available (Feb 2026)**: DeepSeek V3.2, MiniMax M2.1, GLM 4.7, GLM 4.7 Flash, Kimi K2.5, Qwen3 Coder Next — frontier reasoning and agentic coding models. Source: https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/02/amazon-bedrock-adds-support-six-open-weights-models/
**Claude Opus 4.6 and Claude Sonnet 4.6** now listed as featured/available models on Bedrock. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-bedrock.html
**Amazon Nova 2** now listed as a featured model. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-bedrock.html
**Projects API support**: OpenAI-compatible Projects API now supported in Bedrock's Mantle inference engine. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/projects.html

**Server-side tools in Responses API (Jan 2026)**: Responses API now supports server-side tool execution via OpenAI-compatible endpoints, integrating with AgentCore Gateway tools — eliminates need for client-side orchestration. Source: https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/02/amazon-bedrock-server-side-tool-execution-agentcore-gateway/

platforms.name: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
details: **StopRuntimeSession API**: New API action for stopping a runtime session. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/APIReference/API_StopRuntimeSession.html
**Google ADK** added as a supported agent framework. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/using-any-agent-framework.html
**OpenAI Agents SDK** added as a supported agent framework. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/using-any-agent-framework.html
**Google Gemini** added as a supported model. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/using-any-agent-framework.html
**A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol** now supported. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/runtime-a2a.html

**Cedar policy language** explicitly supported in the Policy service as an alternative to natural language policies. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/policy-understanding-cedar.html

platforms.name: ChatGPT Enterprise (OpenAI)
details: **GPT-5.4 Pro and GPT-5.4 Thinking models** launched (Mar 25, 2026). Source: https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-4/
**Open Responses API** made available to the open-source community (Mar 25, 2026). Source: https://community.openai.com/t/open-responses-for-the-open-source-community/1371770
**Playwright MCP integration** added to Codex with approval workflow requirement (Mar 25, 2026). Source: https://developers.openai.com/codex/mcp

**Computer action `keys` field**: New `keys` field added to Click, DoubleClick, Drag, Move, Scroll computer action types in Python SDK v2.30.0 and Node SDK v6.33.0. Source: https://github.com/openai/openai-python/releases

platforms.name: Google Vertex AI
details: **Lyria 3 Pro Music Generation — Public Preview (Mar 25, 2026)**: New music generation model capable of generating full 3-minute tracks with structural awareness (intros, verses, choruses, bridges), upgrading from Lyria 2. Source: https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/music/generate-music

platforms.name: Cursor
details: **Self-hosted Cloud Agents (Mar 25, 2026)**: Run Cursor cloud agents entirely within customer's own network — isolated VMs, full dev environments, multi-model harnesses, and marketplace plugins with full data residency. Source: https://cursor.com/blog/self-hosted-cloud-agents

findings.title: Bedrock AgentCore A2A Protocol Support
findings.description: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore now supports the Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol, enabling inter-agent communication across frameworks. Creates new entity types: A2A sessions, agent-to-agent message flows, and cross-framework agent connections. AISPM must discover and map A2A-enabled agents and their communication patterns to detect unauthorized inter-agent data flows

findings.title: OpenAI GPT-5.4 Pro and Thinking Models
findings.description: OpenAI launched GPT-5.4 Pro and GPT-5.4 Thinking — new frontier model variants with 1M token context, Tool Search capability, and enhanced computer use. AISPM must update model inventory to discover enterprise deployments using these models, track which agents and applications reference them, and ensure governance policies cover the expanded reasoning capabilities of the Thinking variant

findings.title: Bedrock Server-Side Tools in Responses API
findings.description: Amazon Bedrock's Responses API now supports server-side tool execution via AgentCore Gateway, eliminating client-side orchestration. This shifts tool execution from visible client-side calls to opaque server-side execution. AISPM must discover and inventory server-side tool configurations to ensure tool access permissions are tracked

findings.title: Bedrock Projects API (Mantle)
findings.description: OpenAI-compatible Projects API now supported in Bedrock's Mantle inference engine. Introduces new organizational entities (projects) that group models, deployments, and API keys with IAM-based access control and cost tagging. AISPM should discover project resources to map ownership and access boundaries

findings.title: Bedrock AgentCore New Framework and Model Support
findings.description: AgentCore added Google ADK, OpenAI Agents SDK as supported frameworks and Google Gemini as a supported model. Expands the inventory of agent types that can run on AgentCore — AISPM must recognize agents built with these frameworks during discovery

findings.title: Bedrock AgentCore Cedar Policy Language
findings.description: AgentCore Policy service now explicitly supports Cedar as a policy definition language alongside natural language. New entity type: Cedar policy documents attached to agents. AISPM should parse and inventory Cedar policies to assess agent permission boundaries

findings.title: Bedrock New Open-Weight Models
findings.description: Six new open-weight models added to Bedrock: DeepSeek V3.2, MiniMax M2.1, GLM 4.7, GLM 4.7 Flash, Kimi K2.5, Qwen3 Coder Next. AISPM model inventory must be updated to discover and track deployments using these models, especially for compliance with model governance policies

findings.title: OpenAI Open Responses API
findings.description: OpenAI's Responses API is now an open-source specification (backed by Hugging Face) enabling multi-provider interoperability. Enterprises may adopt this as a standard agentic interface across providers. AISPM should monitor for new Responses API-based agent deployments and cross-provider agent configurations

findings.title: Bedrock AgentCore StopRuntimeSession API
findings.description: New `StopRuntimeSession` API action enables programmatic termination of agent runtime sessions. AIDR should monitor calls to this API as they may indicate incident response actions or unauthorized session terminations

findings.title: OpenAI Computer Use Keys Field Expansion
findings.description: OpenAI SDKs (Python v2.30.0, Node v6.33.0) added a `keys` field to all computer action types (Click, DoubleClick, Drag, Move, Scroll), enabling keyboard input during computer use actions. Expands the attack surface for computer-use agents — AIRT should test for keystroke injection and keyboard-based privilege escalation in computer-use scenarios

findings.title: OpenAI Playwright MCP in Codex
findings.description: OpenAI Codex now integrates Playwright MCP with approval workflows. Creates a new browser automation attack surface — AIRT should test for prompt injection via web content during Playwright-driven browsing sessions

findings.title: Cursor Self-Hosted Cloud Agents
findings.description: Cursor now supports running cloud agents entirely within customer networks with isolated VMs, full dev environments, and marketplace plugins. Deployed via Helm chart/Kubernetes operator with fleet management API. Introduces on-premise AI agent infrastructure that needs endpoint monitoring — code execution, tool access, and plugin activity occur within the enterprise perimeter

platforms.name: Azure AI Foundry
details: **Foundry IQ** — New service added to Azure AI Foundry documentation hub. Three new entries: "What is Foundry IQ?", "Frequently asked questions", and "Connect knowledge base to agents". Enables knowledge-base-connected AI agents. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/agents/concepts/what-is-foundry-iq

**Foundry Control Plane** — New top-level documentation section with entries for managing agents, models, and tools; evaluating agentic workflows; and monitoring generative AI applications. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/control-plane/overview
**Explore Foundry Models** — New concept card added to the documentation hub overview. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/foundry/

**Preview Inference API enhancements** — API reference now documents new preview inference API with monthly cadence, highlighting Structured Outputs (JSON schema via response_format), JSON Mode, and log probabilities parameters. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/openai/reference

platforms.name: ChatGPT Enterprise (OpenAI)
details: **Responses API with background mode** — New asynchronous file input processing capability for the Responses API (March 25, 2026). Source: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/background
**gpt-realtime-1.5 GA** — Community confirmation that gpt-realtime-1.5 is now generally available in the Realtime API (March 24, 2026). Source: https://community.openai.com
**ChatGPT Apps SDK** — New SDK actively developing with ui/notifications, tool-result handling, and model context update capabilities. Source: https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk

**MCP Apps Integration** — Model Context Protocol apps now integrating with ChatGPT with tool support and notification systems, expanded from previously seen "MCP Connectors". Source: https://developers.openai.com/apps-sdk/concepts/mcp-server

platforms.name: Amazon Bedrock
details: **9 new foundation models** — Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Opus 4.6, DeepSeek V3.2, Kimi K2.5 (Moonshot AI), MiniMax M2.1, GLM 4.7 (Zhipu), GLM 4.7 Flash (Zhipu), Qwen3 Coder Next (Alibaba), Nova 2 (Amazon). Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/models-supported.html
**3 new model providers** — Moonshot AI, MiniMax, and Zhipu added as Bedrock providers. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/models-supported.html

**OpenAI-compatible Projects API** — New API support in the Mantle inference engine enabling OpenAI API compatibility. Projects provide application-level isolation for GenAI workloads. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/projects.html

**Server-side tools in Responses API** — New tool execution capability using OpenAI API-compatible service endpoints with AgentCore Gateway integration. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/gateway.html

platforms.name: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
details: **InvokeAgentRuntimeCommand API** — New API action for executing shell commands inside running AgentCore Runtime sessions with streaming output. Agents created after March 17, 2026 support this automatically. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/runtime-execute-command.html

**Google ADK and OpenAI Agents SDK** — Two new agent frameworks added to supported frameworks list. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/what-is-bedrock-agentcore.html

**A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol** — New inter-agent communication protocol added to supported protocols. Enables agent discovery via Agent Cards and JSON-RPC communication. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/runtime-a2a-protocol-contract.html
**OpenInference protocol** — New observability protocol added to supported protocols. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/observability.html
**Google Gemini model provider** — New model provider added to AgentCore. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/latest/devguide/what-is-bedrock-agentcore.html

platforms.name: Google Vertex AI
details: **Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview** (Feb 19, 2026) — Advanced reasoning model with 1M token context window. Source: https://docs.cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/models/gemini/3-1-pro
**GLM 4.7 GA** (Jan 20, 2026) — New model with complex reasoning and tool use capabilities, now generally available. Source: https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/release-notes
**Gemini CLI Preview** (Jan 16, 2026) — Open source AI agent for terminal-based interactions with Gemini. Source: https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli

findings.title: Azure Foundry IQ
findings.description: New knowledge-base-connected agent service added to Azure AI Foundry. Introduces new inventory entities: Foundry IQ agents, knowledge base connections, and IQ query sessions. AISPM must discover and map these resources and their data flow to knowledge bases

findings.title: Azure Foundry Control Plane
findings.description: New centralized management layer for agents, models, and tools in Azure AI Foundry. Includes agentic workflow evaluation and GenAI application monitoring. Creates a new governance surface — AISPM should inventory Control Plane-managed resources and track agent-model-tool relationships

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock — 9 new models & 3 new providers
findings.description: Moonshot AI (Kimi K2.5), MiniMax (M2.1), and Zhipu (GLM 4.7/Flash) are entirely new providers. Plus Claude 4.6 variants, DeepSeek V3.2, Qwen3 Coder Next, and Nova 2. AISPM model inventory must be expanded to discover and classify these models and their deployments

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock OpenAI-compatible Projects API
findings.description: New API surface in Mantle inference engine provides OpenAI API compatibility. Creates a parallel invocation path that AISPM must discover — organizations may use this API to invoke Bedrock models without going through the native Bedrock API, bypassing existing monitoring

findings.title: Bedrock AgentCore — A2A protocol & new frameworks
findings.description: Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol enables inter-agent communication, creating new entity relationships. Google ADK and OpenAI Agents SDK are now supported frameworks. AISPM must map A2A connections between agents and discover agents built with these new frameworks

findings.title: Bedrock AgentCore — InvokeAgentRuntimeCommand API
findings.description: New runtime API action for executing shell commands inside agent sessions. Extends the existing AgentCore API surface — AISPM should track calls to this endpoint for agent activity visibility

findings.title: Google Vertex AI — Gemini 3.1 Pro & GLM 4.7 GA
findings.description: New models available in Vertex AI Model Garden. AISPM model inventory for Vertex AI should include these models and track their deployments

findings.title: OpenAI Responses API background mode
findings.description: New asynchronous processing mode enables long-running file-input operations that execute in the background. AIDR must detect and monitor background response sessions — these async operations may process sensitive documents without real-time visibility

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock server-side tools in Responses API
findings.description: New tool execution capability via OpenAI-compatible endpoints with AgentCore Gateway. AIDR must monitor server-side tool invocations — tools executed server-side bypass client-side detection

findings.title: Bedrock AgentCore OpenInference protocol
findings.description: New observability protocol for agent tracing. AIDR can leverage OpenInference traces as a detection data source for monitoring agent behavior

findings.title: OpenAI ChatGPT Apps SDK
findings.description: New SDK for building ChatGPT applications with UI components, notifications, tool-result handling, and model context updates. Creates a new app ecosystem — ENDPOINT must detect ChatGPT Apps running in enterprise environments and monitor their tool usage and data access

findings.title: OpenAI MCP Apps Integration
findings.description: Model Context Protocol apps now integrate directly into ChatGPT with full tool support. MCP apps can access local tools, files, and services — ENDPOINT must discover MCP app connections and monitor the tools they expose to ChatGPT

findings.title: Google Gemini CLI
findings.description: New open-source terminal AI agent for Gemini interactions. ENDPOINT should detect Gemini CLI installations and monitor terminal-based AI agent activity

findings.title: OpenAI MCP Apps — tool execution attack surface
findings.description: MCP apps connecting external tools to ChatGPT introduce prompt injection vectors through tool descriptions and results. AIRT should test MCP app tool chains for injection vulnerabilities and data exfiltration via tool responses

findings.title: Bedrock A2A protocol — inter-agent attack surface
findings.description: Agent-to-Agent communication creates new prompt injection vectors where a compromised agent could manipulate other agents via A2A messages. AIRT should test A2A chains for cross-agent prompt injection and privilege escalation

findings.title: Azure Foundry IQ — knowledge base poisoning surface
findings.description: Foundry IQ connects knowledge bases to agents, creating a data poisoning vector. AIRT should test whether malicious content in knowledge bases can manipulate Foundry IQ agent responses

platforms.name: Microsoft Copilot (M365)
details: **Microsoft Agent 365 (early access preview)** added to the Copilot extensibility developer tools and APIs section. New agent platform entry for building M365-integrated agents. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365-copilot/extensibility/

Graph API: `administrationScopeTargets` relationship added to workflow base resources for targeting specific organizational units in lifecycle workflows (Feb 19, 2026). Source: https://developer.microsoft.com/graph/changelog/

platforms.name: Microsoft Copilot Studio
details: **(Preview) Work IQ tools** — Connect Microsoft 365 Copilot and agents to the Work IQ service for real-time work insights from M365 files, emails, meetings, and chats (March 2026). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/whats-new
**Claude Sonnet 4.5 (beta)** support added for Computer Use agents with improved nuanced decision-making (February 2026). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/whats-new

**Prompt builder enhancements** — Configurable content moderation sensitivity per prompt, new Claude model options (Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.5), inline prompt editing in agent tool details (February 2026). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/whats-new

**Improved agent responses** for ticket-based Microsoft 365 Graph connectors (ServiceNow, Azure DevOps) with better retrieval and actionable summaries (February 2026). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/whats-new

**(Preview) Expanded computer use capabilities** — New model support, built-in credentials, enhanced audit logging with session replay, Cloud PC pooling (January 2026). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/whats-new

**(Preview) Agent evaluation enhancements** — Thumbs-up/down feedback, activity maps for runtime visibility, CSV template for test sets (January 2026). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/whats-new

platforms.name: Azure AI Foundry
details: **Microsoft Foundry rebrand** — Azure AI Foundry documentation rebranded as "Microsoft Foundry" with new sub-services: **Foundry IQ** (knowledge base connections for agents), **Foundry Control Plane** (trustworthy AI, tracing, evaluation, monitoring), **Foundry Local** (run LLMs locally for free). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-studio/

**GPT-Realtime-1.5 and GPT-Audio-1.5** models released with improved instruction following, multi-lingual support, and tool calling for voice-first applications (February 2026). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/openai/whats-new

New preview inference API documented with v1 preview/GA versioning for data plane. Control Plane API updated to latest preview 2025-07-01-preview and GA 2025-06-01. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/openai/reference

platforms.name: Power Apps
details: New **"Code apps"** option added under Create apps section. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-apps/
New **"Overview of the new experience (preview)"** added under AI copilot capabilities (previously called "Vibe"). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-apps/

platforms.name: Amazon Bedrock
details: **Claude Opus 4.6** and **Claude Sonnet 4.6** models now available on Amazon Bedrock (February 2026). Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-bedrock.html

**Six new open weight models** added: DeepSeek V3.2, MiniMax M2.1, GLM 4.7, GLM 4.7 Flash, Kimi K2.5 (Moonshot AI), Qwen3 Coder Next (Alibaba) — focused on frontier reasoning and agentic coding (February 2026). Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-bedrock.html
**Three new model providers** on Bedrock: Moonshot AI, MiniMax, Zhipu. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-bedrock.html
**OpenAI-compatible Projects API** support in the Mantle inference engine. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-bedrock.html

**Server-side tools support in Responses API** using OpenAI API-compatible service endpoints, with AgentCore Gateway tools integration enabling server-side tool execution without client-side orchestration (January 2026). Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-bedrock.html
**Amazon Nova 2** model now featured. Source: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-bedrock.html

platforms.name: Salesforce AgentForce
details: **Spring '26 Agentforce Voice** — New voice-first agent capabilities for customer experiences, including AI agent building for field representatives with voice command support. Source: https://www.salesforce.com/releases/
**Spring '26 Platform/Developer** — Enhanced DevOps capabilities, security improvements, Flow automation scaling enhancements. Source: https://www.salesforce.com/releases/

platforms.name: Google Vertex AI
details: **Partner Model Evaluations** (March 12, 2026) — Gen AI evaluation service now supports assessing partner models including Anthropic and Llama models through console-based workflows. Source: https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/docs/release-notes

platforms.name: Windsurf
details: **Cascade Hooks cloud configuration for enterprises** (v1.9552.21, February 12, 2026) — Enterprise governance feature allowing cloud-managed cascade hook policies. Source: https://windsurf.com/changelog
**MCP auto-triggered OAuth login** (v1.9566.9, February 25, 2026) — MCP refresh button and automatic OAuth login flow for MCP server connections. Source: https://windsurf.com/changelog
**POST_CASCADE_RESPONSE_WITH_TRANSCRIPT cascade hook** (v1.9566.9, February 25, 2026) — New hook type providing full cascade response transcripts. Source: https://windsurf.com/changelog

platforms.name: Dia Browser
details: **v1.22.0** (March 11, 2026), **v1.21.0** (March 5, 2026), **v1.20.0** (February 26, 2026) — Three new releases. Feature details not available from release notes index page. Source: https://diabrowser.com/release-notes

findings.title: Microsoft Agent 365 (Early Access Preview)
findings.description: New agent platform entry added to M365 Copilot extensibility. Introduces a new agent entity type ("Microsoft Agent 365") in the M365 ecosystem that AISPM must discover and inventory alongside existing Copilot extensions, declarative agents, and API plugins. Microsoft Agent 365 is a new control plane for deploying, organizing, and governing agents at scale with integrated identity, compliance, and security from Entra and Defender. GA target: May 1, 2026

findings.title: Copilot Studio Work IQ Tools Integration
findings.description: New service connecting Microsoft 365 Copilot and Copilot Studio agents to the Work IQ service for real-time work insights from M365 files, emails, meetings, and chats. Creates new data flow paths between agents and enterprise productivity data. AISPM must map agent-to-Work-IQ connections and AIDR must monitor data access patterns through this channel

findings.title: Microsoft Foundry Rebrand with New Services (Foundry IQ, Control Plane, Local)
findings.description: Azure AI Foundry rebranded as "Microsoft Foundry" with three new service categories: Foundry IQ (knowledge-base-connected agents, evolved from Azure AI Search), Foundry Control Plane (tracing, evaluation, monitoring), and Foundry Local (local LLM execution on Windows/macOS/Android). Introduces new inventory entities for each service. AISPM must update its Azure AI resource discovery to cover the new Foundry namespace and entity types

findings.title: Copilot Studio Computer Use Expansion
findings.description: Enhanced computer use capabilities with new model support (Claude Sonnet 4.5), built-in credentials management, Cloud PC pooling via Windows 365 for Agents, and enhanced audit logging with session replay. Expands the attack surface for agent-controlled desktop sessions — agents now have credential access and broader desktop control. AIRT should assess prompt injection risks in computer use scenarios and credential exposure vectors

findings.title: Copilot Studio Third-Party Model Support (Claude Sonnet 4.5, Opus 4.6)
findings.description: Copilot Studio prompt builder now supports Claude models (Sonnet 4.5, Opus 4.6) alongside Microsoft models. Agents can use non-Microsoft LLMs, creating new model provider entities to track and new data flow paths to external model providers. AISPM must inventory which agents use which model providers

findings.title: Copilot Studio Configurable Content Moderation Sensitivity
findings.description: Prompt builder now allows configuring content moderation sensitivity per individual prompt (Low/Moderate/High for hate, sexual, violence, self-harm categories). Creates risk of agents with weakened moderation settings. AIDR should detect and flag agents with lowered content moderation thresholds as a violation rule

findings.title: Azure OpenAI GPT-Realtime-1.5 / GPT-Audio-1.5 Models
findings.description: New voice/audio models (gpt-realtime-1.5-2026-02-23 and gpt-audio-1.5-2026-02-23) released on Azure OpenAI for real-time voice-first applications with tool calling support. New model entities for AISPM inventory; voice-enabled agents introduce new interaction channels that expand the monitoring surface

findings.title: Power Apps Code Apps and AI Experience Preview
findings.description: New "Code apps" development mode (now GA) added to Power Apps — enables code-first apps using React/Vue within Power Platform with enterprise governance. Plus a revamped AI copilot experience (preview) via vibe.powerapps.com. New app type entity for AISPM inventory — code apps may have different security characteristics than canvas/model-driven apps

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock Model and Provider Expansion
findings.description: Six new open-weight models (DeepSeek V3.2, MiniMax M2.1, GLM 4.7, GLM 4.7 Flash, Kimi K2.5, Qwen3 Coder Next) and three new providers (Moonshot AI, MiniMax, Zhipu) added to Bedrock, plus Amazon Nova 2. Powered by Project Mantle inference engine. Expands the model inventory AISPM must track and introduces providers from new geographies (China-origin models) with potential data sovereignty implications

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock Server-Side Tools via AgentCore Gateway
findings.description: Responses API now supports server-side tool execution through AgentCore Gateway integration without client-side orchestration. Customers specify an AgentCore Gateway ARN as a tool connector, and Bedrock auto-discovers tools, selects them during inference, and executes server-side — all within a single API call. AISPM must discover these tool configurations and AIDR must monitor server-side tool invocations as a new activity type

findings.title: Amazon Bedrock OpenAI-Compatible Projects API (Mantle)
findings.description: New OpenAI-compatible Projects API surface in the Mantle inference engine (announced Feb 26, 2026). Projects provide logical boundaries for isolating workloads with IAM-based access control and cost tagging. AISPM must discover project entities alongside existing Bedrock resource structures

findings.title: Salesforce Agentforce Voice (Spring '26)
findings.description: New voice-first agent capability delivering natural, human-like conversations aligned to company brand, with real-time CRM record updates, workflow triggering, and API calling — all with full auditability through Data 360. Natively connected to Salesforce with first-class support for Amazon Connect, Five9, Genesys, NiCE, and Vonage. AISPM must inventory voice-enabled Agentforce agents and their data access permissions

findings.title: Google Vertex AI Partner Model Evaluations
findings.description: Gen AI evaluation service now supports assessing third-party partner models (Anthropic, Llama) through console-based workflows via Model Garden. Extends the evaluation surface to non-Google models, which AISPM should track for model governance coverage

findings.title: Windsurf Enterprise Cascade Hooks and MCP OAuth
findings.description: Cloud-configured cascade hooks for enterprise governance (v1.9552.21), auto-triggered OAuth for MCP connections, and new POST_CASCADE_RESPONSE_WITH_TRANSCRIPT hook type (v1.9566.9). Enterprise admin whitelist controls restrict which MCP servers developers can install using regex pattern matching
